page_content:
Scenario 3 of 31 (Questions 11-17)  
You barely passed a course with a lower grade than you would have liked. The professor suggests you enroll in a summer class that will review and expand upon the content covered by the course you completed. The professor notes that the course takes place one day per week and is not required but will help ensure you have a solid foundation for the rest of medical school. You will be on campus this summer, but you had planned to relax and not think about medical school during your break.  
11. Let the professor know your grade does not necessarily reflect your knowledge.  
Very Ineffective  
Half Credit
Ineffective  
Effective  
Very Effective  
Scoring key: Very Ineffective  
This response is very ineffective because it ignores the professor’s recommendation and minimizes the concern about barely passing the course. The student not only fails to take advantage of a learning opportunity—they also demonstrate a lack of open-mindedness about their performance, which risks creating additional problems in future coursework.  
Half credit is awarded to Ineffective because it is one rating away from the key but still on the same side of the scale.  
Question
12. Revisit your schedule to see if you can fit in the summer course while balancing your free time.  
Very Ineffective  
Ineffective  
Effective  
Correct
Very Effective  
Scoring key: Very Effective  
This response is very effective because the student shows an openness to the professor's recommendation while also balancing their own self-care. By exploring ways to prioritize the summer course, the student demonstrates accountability, adaptability, and a commitment to improving their academic foundation.  
Half credit is awarded to Effective because it is one rating away from the key but still on the same side of the scale.  
Question
13. Tell the professor you cannot commit to the summer course at this time.  
Half Credit
Very Ineffective  
Ineffective  
Effective  
Very Effective  
Scoring key: Ineffective  
While the student does not outright reject the professor's academic concerns, declining the summer course does nothing to help the student's performance and is therefore ineffective as an immediate next step. The student should instead consider alternative ways to address this important academic growth opportunity.  
Half credit is awarded to Very Ineffective because it is one rating away from the key but still on the same side of the scale.  
Question
14. Talk with the professor about alternative ways to continue learning the course content without the summer course.  
Very Ineffective  
Incorrect
Ineffective  
Effective  
Very Effective  
Scoring key: Effective  
This response recognizes the need for further learning and seeks potential alternatives. While this could help, the response is not very effective as it disregards the structured support and expertise offered by the summer course that is immediately available.  
Half credit is awarded to Very Effective because it is one rating away from the key but still on the same side of the scale.  
Question
15. Explain to the professor that you do not believe you need additional training given you passed the course.  
Very Ineffective  
Half Credit
Ineffective  
Effective  
Very Effective  
Scoring key: Very Ineffective  
This response is very ineffective because the student disregards the professor’s direct feedback and focuses only on having passed the course. The student demonstrates a lack of insight into their performance and dismisses opportunities for support, which may lead to additional academic challenges in the future.  
Half credit is awarded to Ineffective because it is one rating away from the key but still on the same side of the scale.  
Question
16. Let the professor know you will find other ways to improve your knowledge this summer.  
Very Ineffective  
Correct
Ineffective  
Effective  
Very Effective  
Scoring key: Ineffective  
This response is ineffective because it only offers a vague assurance without engaging with the professor's guidance or exploring tangible alternatives. While the student shows some recognition of the academic concern, the response does not improve the situation as it lacks a concrete plan and overlooks the support system being offered.  
Half credit is awarded to Very Ineffective because it is one rating away from the key but still on the same side of the scale.  
Question
17. Let the professor know you will enroll in the course if they believe it will be helpful.  
Very Ineffective  
Ineffective  
Half Credit
Effective  
Very Effective  
Scoring key: Very Effective  
The student demonstrates trust in the professor's judgment and commitment to academic improvement by accepting the recommendation. By acknowledging and addressing the importance of academic growth, the student demonstrates openness to feedback and a commitment to improving their performance.  
Half credit is awarded to Effective because it is one rating away from the key but still on the same side of the scale.
